member.name: Tonia Antoniazzi
member.synopsis: Tonia Antoniazzi is the Labour MP for Gower , and has been an MP continually since 8 June 2017.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Plastics
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, if her Department will make an assessment of the potential merits of introducing a ban on the import and sale of plastic flying rings.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Tackling marine litter and plastic pollution is a priority for the Government, which is why we are working domestically and internationally to implement measures that will prevent plastic and other litter from reaching the ocean in the first place. The Go...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Plastics: Seals
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether her Department has made an assessment of the potential impact of plastic flying rings on seal populations.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Tackling marine litter and plastic pollution is a priority for the Government, which is why we are working domestically and internationally to implement measures that will prevent plastic and other litter from reaching the ocean in the first place. The Go...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Charities: Women
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps she is taking to ensure that charities for women and girls have updated their charitable purposes in line with the Supreme Court ruling in the case of For Women Scotland v The Scottish
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Charity Commission for England and Wales is working at pace to update its guidance for charities to ensure it aligns with the new code when it is published by Parliament. As charities can have any combination of charitable purposes or stated beneficia...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Cameroon: Politics and Government
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what her Department's policy is on the status of Southern Cameroon following the 2009 African Commission ruling in Gunme v Cameroon.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK has historically recognised the outcome of the 1961 UN-organised plebiscite, in which the territories now forming the North-West and South-West regions voted to join the Republic of Cameroon, as endorsed by the United Nations General Assembly Resol...

writtenQuestions.heading: Cameroon: UN Charter
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what assessment her Department has made of the potential implications for her policies of the absence of a registered Treaty of Union between the Republic of Cameroon and the
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK has historically recognised the outcome of the 1961 UN-organised plebiscite, in which the territories now forming the North-West and South-West regions voted to join the Republic of Cameroon, as endorsed by the United Nations General Assembly Resol...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Cameroon
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of the legal status of Southern Cameroons on the number of asylum applications from that region since 2021.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Home Office has not made a specific assessment that the legal status of those from any part of Cameroon has driven changes in asylum intake since 2021. As with all nationalities, the number of asylum applications can fluctuate for a range of reasons, ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Northern Ireland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, how many asylum claimants there are in Northern Ireland for which the latest data is available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Home Office are unable to state how many asylum claimants there are in Northern Ireland as this information is not routinely collated.The Home Office publishes data on the number of individuals in receipt of asylum support. The most recent published s...

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Northern Ireland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Northern Ireland, what assessment he has made of the potential implications for his policies of trends in the number of asylum seekers in Northern Ireland.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Immigration and asylum policy are reserved matters for the Home Office. I would encourage the Honourable Member to write to the Home Secretary on this matter.

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Belfast
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, how many people made asylum applications at Drumkeen House in Belfast in each of the last three years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information requested is not currently available from published statistics, and the relevant data could only be collated and verified for the purpose of answering this question at disproportionate cost.

writtenQuestions.heading: Dental Health: Antibiotics
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential merits of advising prophylactic antibiotics for those at risk of endocarditis from basic dental examinations.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No assessment has been made by the Department, however the National Institute for Clinical Excellence (NICE) has guidance on the use of antibiotic prophylaxis to prevent infective endocarditis in NICE Clinical Guideline CG64, which can be found at the fol...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Liquefied Petroleum Gas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, whether users of LPG can access the £53 million emergency support package for vulnerable households.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The additional £53 million announced by the government to be targeted at heating oil includes £3.8 million support for Wales, which is being provided to the Welsh Government. It is for the Welsh Government to determine how this funding is allocated and de...

writtenQuestions.heading: Alcoholic Drinks: Marketing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of the potential implications for its policies of the Portman Group’s report entitled Protecting Consumers and Supporting Producers: Regulatory Report 2025,
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has noted the publication of the report Protecting Consumers and Supporting Producers: Regulatory Report 2025, which provides the Portman Groups review of its regulation in 2025.The Portman Group are the alcohol industry’s self-regulatory b...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Expenditure: Northern Ireland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, pursuant to the Answer of 4 February 2026 to Question 118047, which section of the most recent Block Grant Transparency publication details the Barnett consequentials allocated to the Northern Ireland Executive foll
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Barnett formula applies to all changes in UK Government Departmental Expenditure Limits, as set out in the Statement of Funding Policy. The Block Grant Transparency publication breaks down all changes to the Northern Ireland Executive’s block grant fu...

writtenQuestions.heading: Places of Worship Renewal Fund: Wales
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, pursuant to the Answer of 3 February 2026 to Question 109187 and Question 109186 on Religious Buildings: Wales, what additional funding was agreed with the Treasury to support the Places of Worsh
writtenQuestions.answerText: At the Spending Review 2025, HM Treasury agreed budgets for Departments for a three-year period for Resource DEL, and a four-year period for Capital DEL. The Department then completed a Business Planning process to allocate this funding to programmes. Thi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Police Service of Northern Ireland: Expenditure
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Northern Ireland, what estimate he has made of UK Government funding allocated to the PSNI as Additional Security Funding in each financial year since 2011.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Recognising the unique security situation in Northern Ireland, the UK Government makes contributions to the PSNI through Additional Security Funding. The aim of the funding is to enable the PSNI to tackle terrorist threats while ensuring day-to-day polici...

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Expenditure: Northern Ireland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what estimate she has made of the Barnett consequentials allocated to the Northern Ireland Executive from the £750,000 uplift provided in 2019 for an increase in officer numbers to 20,000 in England and Wales.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Barnett formula applies to all changes in UK Government Departmental Expenditure Limits, including the Home Office, as set out in the Statement of Funding Policy. The Block Grant Transparency publication breaks down all changes to the Northern Ireland...

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Expenditure: Northern Ireland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what estimate she has made of the Barnett consequentials allocated to the Northern Ireland Executive following increases in police funding to PCCs in England and Wales in each year since 2020.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Barnett formula applies to all changes in UK Government Departmental Expenditure Limits, as set out in the Statement of Funding Policy. The Block Grant Transparency publication breaks down all changes to the Northern Ireland Executive’s block grant fu...

writtenQuestions.heading: Afghanistan: Women
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what assessment she has made of the potential implications for her policies of the Taliban's decision to permanently ban women and girls from education.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer provided on 12 January in response to Question 103187.

writtenQuestions.heading: Religious Buildings: Wales
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps she is taking to secure adequate funding opportunities for places of worship in Wales.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Places of Worship Renewal Fund is focused solely on England because heritage policy is devolved. Future capital funding for Wales is supported through Barnett allocations, which provide proportional funding to the Welsh Government. As these funds are ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Religious Buildings: Wales
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, if she will make an assessment of the potential impact of recently announced changes to capital funding for places of worship on places of worship in Wales.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Places of Worship Renewal Fund is focused solely on England because heritage policy is devolved. Future capital funding for Wales is supported through Barnett allocations, which provide proportional funding to the Welsh Government. As these funds are ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport
